§6Structure Tower§f:
The §6Structure Tower §f(advancedrocketry:structuretower) is a §emultiblock placeholder block §f used to define the §6shape and size §f of Advanced Rocketry §6multiblocks§f. It is the §6scaffolding §f that machines use to determine their §6bounding box§f.

§6Production Path (recipes/structuretower.json)§f:
Pattern "xxx / x / xxx" (a 3×3 minus centre and middle edges):
- §bx§f: §6Steel Stick §f(stickSteel) — 8 (5 in the corners + 1 top middle + 1 bottom middle + 1 left + 1 right, no centre)
- Yields 8× Structure Tower

§6Function§f:
- §bMultiblock scaffolding §f: §binvisible §fblock that defines a §6multiblock's structure §f in AR
- §bInert §f: has no §6functionality §f of its own; does not connect to power or items
- §bBlast resistance §f: §b30 §f — standard
- §bUsed by §f: all Advanced Rocketry §6multiblock machines §f
- §bPlayer placement §f: in §6creative §f, can be placed as a §bdebug tool §f; in §6survival §f, it appears §bautomatically §f when an AR multiblock is built

§6Direct Descendants (TaskRetrieval graph)§f:
The jei-graph shows 19 items the Structure Tower is a component of, including:
- §6Lathe §f(advancedrocketry:lathe)
- §6Rolling Machine §f(advancedrocketry:rollingmachine)
- §6Cutting Machine §f(advancedrocketry:cuttingmachine)
- §6Precision Assembler §f(advancedrocketry:precisionassemblingmachine)
- §6Centrifuge §f(advancedrocketry:centrifuge)
- §6Electrolyser §f(advancedrocketry:electrolyser)
- §6Black Hole Generator §f(advancedrocketry:blackholegenerator)
- §6Oxygen Scrubber §f(advancedrocketry:oxygenscrubber)

§6Practical Use§f:
- §eTip§f: most §6multiblock machines §f are §b3×3×3 §f hollow cubes with §6Structure Towers §f on the §b8 corners §f
- §eTip§f: do §cnot §f break a §6Structure Tower §f while a §6multiblock §f is active — it will §cdismantle §f the multiblock
- §cWarning§f: the §6Structure Tower §f is §6invisible §f in many rendering modes; use §6F3 + B §f to see hitboxes
- §cWarning§f: a misplaced §6Structure Tower §f can cause §bghost blocks §f in a multiblock; double-check placement
